MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Jul-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
Off Calendar Dec-04-2013 Off Calendar

Defendant Henry Alvarez, Iii'S Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Dec-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Henry Alvarez, III'S Motion To Change Venue is denied. In his memorandum of points and authorities, Defendant moves for change of venue pursuant to CCP 392. It is apparent that Defendant means CCP 397(b) ("When there is reason to believe that an impartial trial cannot be had therein.") Defendant has not met his burden of showing that a change of venue is warranted. (See Nguyen v. Superior Court (1996) 49 Cal. App. 4th 1781, 1791.) The anecdotal evidence in the form of a limited number of news articles -- the most recent of which is from May 2013 - are insufficient to show that defendant cannot receive an impartial jury trial. Defendant's characterization of a handful of articles as "viciously negative" and an "editorial pogrom" is not sufficient to demonstrate a "widespread feeling of prejudice extending over a long time." (Nguyen, supra, at 1791.) The statistical evidence, in the form of an on-line survey, is also insufficient. The data is not a random sample, it asked leading questions, and there is no way to determine whether the respondents were a representative sample of San Francisco jurors. Further, the on-line survey failed to ask whether the respondents could be fair and impartial jurors. The Court credits the declaration of Alex Evans in opposition to the motion to transfer, which sets forth the methodological flaws in defendant's survey evidence. Further, even if the evidence in the on-line survey were meaningful (and the Court finds on balance that it is not), the data is subject to different interpretations and conclusions. Defendant's polling expert says "40% of those polled had seen or heard some media coverage of the SFHA prior to the poll." (Bradley Declaration in support of Motion, 7.) That means that 60% had not. Finally, the experience in the recent Myers trial, as anecdotally relayed by Defendant, does not suggest that Defendant could not have an impartial trial in this County. It is a commonplace to instruct jurors not to read any media coverage, whether in print or on-line, and to take steps to ensure that this order is complied with during trial if circumstances suggest that the order might have been violated. Overall, considering all of the evidence, Defendant has not shown that he could not have an impartial trial in San Francisco in 2014. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
